name: Electron electric dipole moment below JILA limit
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict an electron electric dipole moment |d_e| below the JILA HfF+ ion-trap upper limit. Like the neutron EDM, a nonzero d_e signals CP violation beyond the SM. The bound is currently the most stringent test of CP-violating new physics in the lepton sector.

regime.description: Electron-scale; sensitive to multi-TeV CP-violating new physics depending on couplings.

current_bound.reference: Roussy et al., 'An improved bound on the electron's electric dipole moment', Science 381, 6653 (2023), arXiv:2212.11841

what_it_excludes: Together with the neutron EDM, severely constrains CP-violating new physics at the multi-TeV scale. Generic SUSY with O(1) phases at LHC-accessible masses is heavily constrained; many leptoquark, two-Higgs-doublet, and extended-gauge models with natural CP phases are excluded or pushed to high scales. The electron-EDM bound is the most stringent test of leptonic CP violation.

parameterization.name: SMEFT dipole and four-fermion CP-violating Wilson coefficients in the electron sector: Im(C_eB^11), Im(C_eW^11), and four-fermion operators contributing to the electron EDM via mixing.
parameterization.reference: Engel, Ramsey-Musolf, van Kolck, PPNP 71, 21 (2013); Cesarotti, Lu, Nakai, Parikh, Reece, JHEP 05, 059 (2019), arXiv:1810.07736.
parameterization.summary: The electron EDM is dominantly produced by the photon dipole operator coefficient C_egamma = c_W C_eB - s_W C_eW evaluated at the electroweak scale, with contributions from two-loop Barr-Zee diagrams when CP-violating Higgs-like states are present.

procedural.predicted_observable: d_e = (v / sqrt(2)) * Im(C_egamma^11) / Lambda^2 + two-loop contributions from CP-violating Yukawas and Higgs sector.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: |Im(C_egamma^11) / Lambda^2| < ~2 x 10^-12 GeV^-2, i.e. |Im(C_egamma)| < O(10^-6) for Lambda = 1 TeV.
framework_inputs: If the framework predicts CP violation in the lepton sector, compute its contribution to the electron dipole Wilson coefficients (one-loop matching) plus dominant two-loop Barr-Zee diagrams.
Include any RG running between the matching scale and the electron scale.
procedural.notes: Companion to neutron_electric_dipole_moment.json: together they probe CP violation in the lepton and quark sectors. Future improvements: ACME III, advanced YbOH and BaF molecular experiments aim for ~10^-30 - 10^-31 e cm sensitivity.

primary_references.citation: Roussy et al., 'An improved bound on the electron's electric dipole moment', Science 381, 6653 (2023)
Cesarotti, Lu, Nakai, Parikh, Reece, 'Interpreting the Electron EDM Constraint', JHEP 05, 059 (2019)
